research.interface: ServiceNow REST Attachment API, /api/now/attachment (unversioned = latest; Australia release documentation)

official_sources.title: Attachment API - ServiceNow API reference (Australia release)
official_sources.used_for: current-release confirmation of POST /api/now/attachment/upload multipart contract: form fields table_name/table_sys_id, file part named uploadFile with the file's own Content-Type, 201 result envelope; and the binary POST /api/now/attachment/file variant with table_name/table_sys_id/file_name query params

official_sources.title: Attachment API - ServiceNow API reference (Zurich release)
official_sources.used_for: full endpoint list (GET /api/now/attachment, GET /{sys_id}, GET /{sys_id}/file, POST /file, POST /upload, DELETE /{sys_id}), attachment metadata fields (sys_id, file_name, table_name, table_sys_id, size_bytes, content_type, download_link, ...), status codes 201/400/401/404/500

research.language_rationale: ServiceNow ships no official JavaScript/TypeScript SDK for the Attachment API; the documented integration surface is REST multipart upload, and TypeScript on Node's built-in fetch/http stack is the idiomatic zero-dependency way to build tooling uploaders.

verified_facts: The multipart upload endpoint is POST /api/now/attachment/upload; the request Content-Type is multipart/form-data, the form fields table_name and table_sys_id name the target record, and the file travels in a form part named uploadFile whose own Content-Type must describe the file being attached.

The binary alternative is POST /api/now/attachment/file with required query parameters table_name, table_sys_id and file_name, the raw bytes as the request body, and the file's MIME type as the request Content-Type.

Successful uploads return 201 with the attachment metadata record in a {"result": ...} envelope; metadata includes sys_id, file_name, table_name, table_sys_id, size_bytes, content_type and download_link, with size_bytes serialized as a string.

GET /api/now/attachment lists attachment metadata and accepts sysparm_query/sysparm_limit, which lets a client correlate attachments by table_name, table_sys_id and file_name before or after an upload.

Documented upload status codes are 201, 400 (missing/invalid parameters), 401, 404 (target record not found) and 500; error responses use the {"error": {"message", "detail"}, "status": "failure"} envelope.

Inbound REST rate limiting answers 429 with a Retry-After header in seconds; the retry budget and the ambiguous-success recovery rule (re-query the correlation key before ever re-POSTing) are local policies pinned in docs/contract.json.
